title: End of Evangelion Pre Production Screenplays
display_title: End of Evangelion Pre Production Screenplays
wikitext:
[]: End of Evangelion (Pre-Production) Screenplays             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 21, 2009 3:26 am You know, the things that "Last A" and "Last B" are from. I've put up a page at the Wiki describing what these screenplays are, archiving the original Japanese text, and providing the oft-reposted link to MDWigs' page. For your fun and perusal, I've started up a list of differences between screenplay and final (to be gradually added to). Plus some new translation work. Though, I'll have to tell you more about the rainbow-colored Spear of Longinus when I return from my self-imposed temporary forum exile. The "HOW DARE YOU HARM THIS BEAUTIFUL BODY" part would have been so awesome. (If the Eva were the one saying it.)

[]:             Quote Post by RockManBalls » Tue Apr 21, 2009 8:21 am Wow, I remember hearing about this eons ago... Thanks so much for archiving it all. Jesus... Last B's ending scares the crap out of me. I would've preferred either ending than what we got, personally.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Tue Apr 21, 2009 12:33 pm That gives me an awesome idea for a fanfic....

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Apr 21, 2009 1:10 pm I can't help but wonder if for this scene Asuka Versus Eva Series (4) that M-8 Tragic Battle - Asuka's Death - Religious (Unused piece on S2 Works )* was supposed to play (although I think the chorus delay version would have been fitting right when Eva-02 is speared). Speaking of which, I guess the screenplays say nothing about the script with character descriptions or what BGM plays when? Very cool to see this information up instead of just the juicy tidbits Wigsy had on his site ages ago. Thanks Reichu! *Am I the only one who loves some of the unused stuff and hopes that SOMETHING from it is sneaked into Rebuild?

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Tue Apr 21, 2009 9:43 pm Screenplay translation dongle wrote: (This is, again, a straight image of rape.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 23, 2009 2:49 pm I said I wasn't going to post anything for a while, but retardation in the Rebuild forum made me mad, so I'm going to post this and then disappear for real. I swear. After I update the Wiki page with that insane “there’s a Kaworu growing out of me thumb!” Gendo scene tonight, anyway… RockManBalls wrote: Wow, I remember hearing about this eons ago... Thanks so much for archiving it all.

[]:             Quote Post by RockManBalls » Thu Apr 23, 2009 7:53 pm I think 上乗り is just referring to the MP Evas being "on top of" Unit-02. The Japanese have a long memory and it's a bit common to exclude the implied subject, which is Unit-02, if you've already referred to it earlier. It makes sense to write [弐号機の]上乗りのまま頭を押されられ, right? "そのまま上乗りにされ" "When [Unit-02/Asuka] is being pinned down," and "上乗りのまま頭を押されられ" "As she's pinned, an Eva plants down 02's face." Mama and sono mama reinforce Unit-02 still being pinned because they generally refer to a condition or state which has stayed the same. It's a bit pointless to change what you've already written as it doesn't really translate anyhow. Also, I looked over Misato's dying speech with Shinji (SPOILERS) and the only difference I noticed was she told him to 帰って来る, instead of (I think it was) 戻って来る in the final version. Small difference, but 帰って is a bit more familial, like come home, or come back to me. Come back to Cold Mountain, Inman. Come back to me.

[]: Last A & Last B             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Wed Apr 29, 2009 5:18 pm On the regards of the Last A and Last B "alternate endings" to EoE, were they ever fully animated and if so where could I view them??? Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Apr 29, 2009 5:26 pm No, they were simply in script/note format.

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Wed Apr 29, 2009 6:04 pm That sucks they would've been badass if put to film.

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Wed Apr 29, 2009 8:40 pm Reichu wrote: I guess it's possible; it sure as heck isn't what we'd think of as an "object of art". Kind of goes along with how I still have not "figured out" the way the word "image" (ime-ji) is employed in these scripts.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 30, 2009 12:44 pm Reichu wrote: What stuff do you “love”, out of curiosity?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 30, 2009 4:18 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Something from proposal, I'm guessing?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 15, 2009 8:51 am RockManBalls wrote: I think 上乗り is just referring to the MP Evas being "on top of" Unit-02.

[]:             Quote Post by RockManBalls » Sat May 16, 2009 11:59 am Misato's monologue in the car: "And then the Eva Series. They still haven't given an order to dismantle them. That is because they intend to use them for their own scenario; for what the committee intends to do next/after/now that the angels are defeated (I guess). Not using Lilith, but Eva." I think there's supposed to be a brief pause between 委員会の and その後のシナリオに. その解体処分の命は, an order to dismantle/dispose of. 未だどこにもない, there is none, nowhere in sight. 未だ is also "mada" in case that part was confusing. Right before Misato's "Angels and Humans" speech: 主観っぽく I believe is referring to a first-person view. I mean, I don't know much about film or art direction, but it sounds like a first-person shot from Misato's car as it exits the tunnel. Or something... From the MP fight: I believe it's making an overture that the whole ordeal is to be a metaphor for rape. Using sutoreeto and imeji makes whoever wrote it sound sort of pretentious, but I imagine it's a vague reference to how they want it to look rape-y. From shortly after Rei returns to Lilith, and Gendo is alone down in T.D.: Methinks it's supposed to be written 人面瘡, jinmensou, a Japanese horror obsession of a tumor or piece of skin with a person's face. They've just used katakana instead of a complicated kanji I guess. So 人面ソの様に, "like a jinmensou." The rest you've got right, his/Kaworu's torso, which I'm guessing is supposed to symbolize Adam, comes out of Gendo's hand or base of his thumb whatever you call that thing. I'm a little confused though. Rei takes embyro Adam from his right hand, and Gendo then places his left hand on the wound which then, I guess from touching some adam/Rei/lilith juice, starts going crazy? Do I have that right? Because that would've made an incredible visual. Right after that: The first sentence is a fragment so I'm thinking there's supposed to be a slight pause in between kore wa and hajimatte no ka. I could be wrong though: "Has it... begun? This isn't Rei (as in, this is not Rei's doing). Yui? Is it... Shinji? Is Shinji doing this?" My lord, all of this sounds unnecessarily awesome. Why the hell was none of this included in the final cut? Rebuild needs to have 3.0 be a wiping out of the rest of the angels and lengthy flashbacks to the origin of the characters and Nerv and 4.0 needs to be this movie that was never made. Again, thanks for your hard work, it's a really fun and sort of weird journey to read this stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat May 16, 2009 1:19 pm RockManBalls wrote: I think there's supposed to be a brief pause between 委員会の and その後のシナリオに. その解体処分の命は, an order to dismantle/dispose of. 未だどこにもない, there is none, nowhere in sight. 未だ is also "mada" in case that part was confusing.

[]:             Quote Post by RockManBalls » Mon May 18, 2009 7:20 pm Reichu wrote: How exactly ARE "straight" and "image" being used here, anyway? It doesn't seem to bear much resemblance to English usage. "Image" in particular gets used lots (LOTS) in these screenplays/scripts, and it's always been a bit on the confusing side.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Mon Sep 12, 2011 11:48 am Anyone feel like doing more translating? The mystifying playground scene seems to have more material in the draft (http://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources_Talk:End_of_Evangelion_Screenplays#Playground_scene) and in particular, I think the last two school bits might shed light on how the cello bit (http://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Sandbox_Sequence#.22It.27s_just_like_when_I_started_the_cello.22).

[]:             Quote Post by 1731298478 » Mon Sep 12, 2011 7:11 pm gwern wrote: Anyone feel like doing more translating? The mystifying playground scene seems to have more material in the draft (http://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources_Talk:End_of_Evangelion_Screenplays#Playground_scene) and in particular, I think the last two school bits might shed light on how the cello bit (http://wiki.evageeks.org/Theory_and_Analysis:Sandbox_Sequence#.22It.27s_just_like_when_I_started_the_cello.22).

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Sep 13, 2011 1:14 am That was terribly depressing! Kind of wish they'd animated it.
